mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is particularly useful for deploying large language models efficiently and reliably in production environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for appropriate hardware resources to maximize performance.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command line interface, which is necessary for managing deployments.

```bash

curl -Lo llm-d https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d && sudo mv llm-d /usr/local/bin/

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to a directory in your PATH.
commands: curl -Lo llm-d https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d && sudo mv llm-d /usr/local/bin/

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in the desired region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust the region and node count as necessary for your requirements.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig to use the new cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for your llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies the autoscaler configuration.
> This command sets up monitoring for your deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment from your cluster. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs.

```bash

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/all.yaml

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command deletes the entire cluster and its resources.
commands: gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your configurations and data. This process involves draining nodes, upgrading the deployment, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/all.yaml --version v0.5.1

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get pods

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node you want to drain.
> This command upgrades the deployment to the specified version.
> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node you want to uncordon.
> Verify that all pods are running and ready after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates that the image is not available or the repository is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it exists in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough CPU resources to schedule the pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or the machine type in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This often happens due to misconfiguration or missing environment variables.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the service is not reachable, possibly due to misconfigured networking.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration and ensure that the correct ports are exposed.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new accelerators, improved inference scheduling algorithms, and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods' showing all pods in the 'Running' state, indicating that llm-d is successfully deployed.
codeSnippets: curl -Lo llm-d https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d && sudo mv llm-d /usr/local/bin/
gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the necessary CLI tools installed.
